title: mls: no per-client cap on number of MLS groups — unbounded HashMap growth
description: core/src/mls.rs MlsClient stores all active groups in a HashMap<Vec<u8>, MlsGroup> with no cap (line 59). In the relay CLI/daemon each GROUP_CREATE call adds an entry. Since MlsGroup holds full MLS ratchet state (tree, key schedule, epoch secrets), a hostile operator could create thousands of relay groups and force every connected client's MlsClient to grow its map unboundedly. There is no maximum on number of groups per MlsClient instance. Fix: impose a cap on groups.len() in create_group_with_id and join_from_welcome_for_group, returning an error when the cap is exceeded.
notes: Part of security review round 23 (epic nie-nvk2)
